description_long: This object is a classic lever-action shotgun with a long, blued steel barrel and a polished wooden stock. The barrel is slender and extends significantly from the receiver, with a simple, unvented design and no visible ribbing. The receiver and lever mechanism are made of dark metal, contrasting with the reddish-brown wood of the stock and fore-end. The stock is a traditional full stock, smoothly contoured and finished, providing a comfortable shoulder rest. The trigger guard is rounded and encloses the trigger securely, with a lever loop integrated beneath it for cycling the action. The overall design is reminiscent of late 19th to early 20th-century repeating shotguns, suitable for both utility and display in urban or rural settings.
description: A lever-action shotgun with a long blued barrel, polished wooden full stock, and a rounded trigger guard with an integrated lever loop.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the wooden stock, long barrel, and lever-action mechanism.
description_view_1: Front-on view emphasizing the narrow barrel profile and the alignment of the stock behind it.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the lever and trigger guard.
description_view_3: Rear angled view focusing on the stock and the top of the receiver, with the lever and barrel partially visible.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long and unvented with no visible ribbing
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with an integrated lever loop and smooth contours
